name: New Guinea Singing Dog
description: The New Guinea Singing Dog is one of the rarest and most primitive of all canines, named for its extraordinary howling vocalization that involves harmonics and multi-tonal sounds not produced by any other dog breed or wild canid. Isolated in the highland forests of New Guinea for thousands of years, this breed is closely related to the Australian Dingo and represents one of the earliest branches of the domestic dog family tree. The captive population consists of fewer than 300 dogs, all descended from a handful of animals captured in the 1950s and 1970s, and a small wild population was recently rediscovered in the remote highlands of Indonesian Papua.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring
